candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture be made non-circular by defining 'knowledge-bearing states,' 'records,' and 'accessible information asymmetry' in purely microphysical or information-theoretic terms, without already presupposing a past/future distinction? The key tension is whether the proposal genuinely explains the arrow of time or merely redescribes entropy increase and record formation in temporal language.

What exact dynamical and statistical conditions make record accumulation generic in one direction from a low-entropy boundary, rather than just probable in a hand-wavy sense? This includes whether one can derive a quantitative criterion for when reversible microdynamics produce robust, mutually consistent records, observers, and computation, and whether this differs in substance from the standard Past Hypothesis account.

How should the conjecture handle cosmological cases with multiple boundaries or atypical global structure—such as two-time-boundary universes, recollapsing cosmologies, de Sitter fluctuations, or local entropy reversals—where different regions might support different 'record directions'? The open question is whether the proposed epistemic arrow is globally unique, locally emergent, or potentially fragmented across the universe.
